title: Taraxacum hjeltii
intro:
There is not enough information on Taraxacum hjeltii to write a paragraph about it. However I can tell you about Taraxacum officinale, also known as the common dandelion or Taraxacum.

Taraxacum officinale is a perennial plant in the sunflower family, characterized by its jagged, toothed leaves and bright yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, and its health benefits include treating digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and acting as a diuretic. The bioactive constituents of Taraxacum officinale include inulin, taraxasterol, and sesquiterpene lactones, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Taraxacum officinale include tea, capsules, and tinctures, often used to support liver and digestive health.
taxonomy: Taraxacum hjeltii is classified as a species within the genus Taraxacum, which is a group of dandelion plants. This classification places it in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. Within the family Asteraceae, Taraxacum hjeltii is further classified as belonging to the subfamily Cichorioideae, which is a group of plants that includes various types of thistles and chicory. At the order level, Taraxacum hjeltii is classified as an Asterales plant, which is a group of flowering plants that includes various types of sunflowers, daisies, and asters. The kingdom and phylum classification of Taraxacum hjeltii places it within the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta.
